data.title: Three Chinese nationals killed in Tajik-Afghan border attack
data.description:
BEIJING (Reuters) -Three Chinese citizens in Tajikistan were killed in an armed attack on the Central Asian country's border with Afghanistan, China's embassy in Dushanbe said on Friday.

One other Chinese national was injured in the attack, which took place on Wednesday evening in Tajikistan's southwestern Khatlon province, the embassy said, urging its citizens to evacuate the border area.

The embassy did not say who was behind the attack, but said China had urged Tajikistan to investigate.

Tajikistan's foreign ministry said in a statement on Thursday the attack targeted the company LLC Shahin SM and had been carried out from Afghanistan using an unmanned aerial vehicle equipped with grenades. The three people who died were all employees of the company, it said.

There was no immediate response from the administration in Kabul.

(Reporting by Joe Cash; Editing by Kate Mayberry)

Vietnam Signs 5G Deals with Huawei and ZTE - Reuters
data.description:
Chinese telecom giants Huawei and ZTE have secured several smaller 5G contracts in Vietnam this year, according to sources cited by Reuters.

The deals signal a clearer shift toward China at a time when relations with the United States have weakened following the introduction of new tariffs on Vietnamese goods.

A consortium including Huawei won a contract in April valued at approximately $23 million, while ZTE has recently secured two deals totaling just over $20 million.

The news has raised concerns among Western officials, who warn that Chinese technology could undermine trust in Vietnam's networks and jeopardize the country's access to advanced American technology.

Ericsson and Nokia, together with Qualcomm, have previously won contracts in the 5G sector.

U.K. Car Manufacturing Falls After Cyberattack on Jaguar Land Rover
data.description:
By Ian Walker


U.K. car manufacturing fell in October as Jaguar Land Rover restarted operations following the cyberattack in August that disrupted its production lines, according to an industry body.

The Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders said Friday that 59,010 cars rolled off U.K. production lines in October compared with 77,484 for the same month last year. This takes the year-to-date figure to 602,109 cars compared with 670,346 for the same period a year earlier.

Of those cars, 46% were either battery electric, plug-in hybrid or hybrid, with volumes up 10% to 27,287 units, the SMMT said.

Some 3,106 commercial vehicles were produced in the month compared with 12,387 in October 2024, the SMMT said.

Jaguar--owned by India's Tata Motors--discovered a cyberattack in late August, forcing the company to shut down its computer systems and halt production. It restarted production on Oct. 8.


Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-28-25 0044ET

Rescuers step up recovery operations as Southeast Asia flood deaths reach 129
data.description:
JAKARTA/BANGKOK (Reuters) -The death toll from floods across large swathes of Southeast Asia rose to at least 129 on Friday, with authorities in the region working to rescue stranded citizens, restore power and communications and coordinate recovery efforts as the waters began to recede.

Large parts of Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand have been stricken by cyclone-fuelled torrential rain for a week, with a rare tropical storm forming in the Malacca Strait.    

On badly hit Sumatra in Indonesia, 72 people had been confirmed dead by Friday morning, said Abdul Muhari, spokesman for Indonesia's national disaster mitigation agency.

Communications remained down in some parts of the island, and authorities were working to restore power and clear roads that have been blocked by landslide debris, he said. 

Indonesia will continue to airlift aid and rescue personnel into stricken areas on Friday, he added.

Thai authorities said the bodies of at least 55 people killed by floods were found in the southern province of Songkhla.

In the city of Hat Yai in Songkhla, the rain had finally stopped on Friday, but residents were still ankle-deep in flood waters and many remained without electricity as they assessed the damage done to their property over the last week. One said he had "lost everything". 

In Malaysia, where two people have been confirmed dead, tropical storm Senyar made landfall at around midnight and has since weakened. Meteorological authorities are still bracing themselves for heavy rain and winds, and warned that rough seas could pose risks for small boats. 

A total of 30,000 evacuees remain in shelters, down from more than 34,000 on Thursday.   

Malaysia's foreign ministry said on Friday that it had already evacuated 1,459 Malaysian nationals stranded in more than 25 flood-hit hotels in neighbouring Thailand, adding that it would work to rescue the remaining 300 still caught up in flood zones.  

(Reporting by Stanley Wisianto in Jakarta, Danial Azhar in Kuala Lumpur, Panarat Thepgumpanat in Bangkok; Writing by David Stanway; Editing by Raju Gopalakrishnan)

data.title: YHLO Biotech Gets Dutch Certification for HIV Kit
Indonesia rejects US 'poison pill' clauses in trade deal talks, FT reports
data.description:
(Reuters) -Indonesia is resisting U.S. efforts to include "poison pill" clauses in a reciprocal tariff trade deal, challenging Washington's push to curb China's influence in Southeast Asia, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the negotiations.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

(Reporting by Rajveer Singh Pardesi in Bengaluru; Editing by Tom Hogue)

JP Morgan Raises Novonesis Target Price to 560 Danish Kroner, Reiterates Overweight Rating - BN
data.description: JP Morgan has increased its target price for Novonesis to 560 Danish kroner, up from the previous 512. The bank also reiterated its "Overweight" rating on the stock, according to Bloomberg News (BN).

data.title: Goldman Sachs Raises Price Target for Norsk Hydro to 75 Norwegian Kroner, Maintains Neutral Rating - BN
data.description: Goldman Sachs has raised its price target for Norsk Hydro to 75 Norwegian kroner, up from the previous target of 73 kroner. The investment bank maintains its neutral rating on the stock, according to Bloomberg News (BN).
